== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8210005005734 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福井県大野市篠座42-5', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:篠座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11603763}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shinokura Shrine | 画像=[[File:shinokurashrinehaidenwinter.jpg|270px|Shinokura Shrine (Ōno City)]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q385684|WD=Q385684|1=Ōno, Fukui}} Shinokura 42-5 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|58|17|N|136|29|19|E|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi Ōkami}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Yōrō (era)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yōrō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=717|Q23532|WD=Q23532|1=717}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} / {{ill|lt=April 21|Q2525|WD=Q2525|lt=21st|1=April 21}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Shinokura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Shinokura Shrine''' (しのくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shinokura, {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q385684|WD=Q385684|1=Ōno, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *'''{{ill|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi Ōkami}}''' (おおなむちのおおかみ) === Associated enshrinements === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} (すくなひこなのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (ほんだわけのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine history, in {{ill|lt=Yōrō (era)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yōrō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=717|Q23532|WD=Q23532|1=717}}), in March, monk {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} set out from Asōzu intending to ascend {{ill|lt=Mount Haku|Q2639098|WD=Q2639098|1=Mount Haku}} and, upon arriving in Ōno, stayed for about ten days at a place south of the shrine where clear spring water flowed from woodland. He reached the summit of Hakusan on {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} of the same year, and when he returned to Shinokura in September, he received an oracle: “I am Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. This is an excellent woodland-spring place where I always delight my heart and descend to wander.” A shrine was then established, which is taken as the origin of enshrinement. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Fukui)|Q8081656|WD=Q8081656|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno District, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Echizen Province|Q1047144|WD=Q1047144|1=Echizen Province}}, and ranked as a small shrine. {{ill|lt=Mount Iiburi|Q11666745|WD=Q11666745|1=Ifuriyama}}, rising behind the shrine, is also called “Otake-san” and is regarded as an ancient {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}. On the {{ill|lt=vernal equinox|Q1339268|WD=Q1339268|1=Chunfen}} and {{ill|lt=autumnal equinox|Q1200436|WD=Q1200436|1=Qiufen}}, the sun sets at the summit on the extension line of the torii and shrine building. [[ファイル:Higanshinokurashrine.jpg|サムネイル|Sunset over Mt. Iiburi as seen from Shinokura Shrine]] === Access === *15 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Echizen-Ōno Station|Q2815910|WD=Q2815910|1=Echizen-Ōno Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kuzuryū Line|Q5357982|WD=Q5357982|1=Etsumi-Hoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}. *From Echizen-Ōno Station, get off at the “Shinokura Shrine Sandō-guchi” stop on {{ill|lt=Ōno municipal bus #Blue Bus|Q108060249|WD=Q108060249|1=Ōno municipal bus #Blue Bus}}, then immediate walk. *From {{ill|lt=Fukui Station (Fukui)|Q867992|WD=Q867992|lt=Fukui Station|1=Fukui Station (Fukui)}} or {{ill|lt=Katsuyama Station|Q11400269|WD=Q11400269|1=Katsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keifuku Bus|Q6383575|WD=Q6383575|1=Keifuku Bus}} and get off at “Vio,” then 5 minutes on foot east. === External links === *[http://www.shinokura.net/ Shinokura Shrine] (official site) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinokura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinokura Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:History of Ono, Fukui]] [[Category:大野市の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8210005005734 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福井県大野市篠座42-5', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:篠座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11603763}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Shinokura Shrine | 画像=[[File:shinokurashrinehaidenwinter.jpg|270px|Shinokura Shrine (Ōno City)]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q385684|WD=Q385684|1=Ōno, Fukui}} Shinokura 42-5 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|58|17|N|136|29|19|E|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi Ōkami}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Yōrō (era)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yōrō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=717|Q23532|WD=Q23532|1=717}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} / {{ill|lt=April 21|Q2525|WD=Q2525|lt=21st|1=April 21}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Shinokura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Shinokura Shrine''' (しのくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Shinokura, {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q385684|WD=Q385684|1=Ōno, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=prefectural shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *'''{{ill|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi Ōkami}}''' (おおなむちのおおかみ) === Associated enshrinements === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687}} (すくなひこなのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (ほんだわけのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (このはなさくやひめのみこと) === History === According to shrine history, in {{ill|lt=Yōrō (era)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yōrō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=717|Q23532|WD=Q23532|1=717}}), in March, monk {{ill|lt=Taichō|Q2388439|WD=Q2388439|1=Taichō}} set out from Asōzu intending to ascend {{ill|lt=Mount Haku|Q2639098|WD=Q2639098|1=Mount Haku}} and, upon arriving in Ōno, stayed for about ten days at a place south of the shrine where clear spring water flowed from woodland. He reached the summit of Hakusan on {{ill|lt=June 18|Q2670|WD=Q2670|1=June 18}} of the same year, and when he returned to Shinokura in September, he received an oracle: “I am Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto. This is an excellent woodland-spring place where I always delight my heart and descend to wander.” A shrine was then established, which is taken as the origin of enshrinement. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Fukui)|Q8081656|WD=Q8081656|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno District, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Echizen Province|Q1047144|WD=Q1047144|1=Echizen Province}}, and ranked as a small shrine. {{ill|lt=Mount Iiburi|Q11666745|WD=Q11666745|1=Ifuriyama}}, rising behind the shrine, is also called “Otake-san” and is regarded as an ancient {{ill|lt=shintai-zan|Q1595289|WD=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}. On the {{ill|lt=vernal equinox|Q1339268|WD=Q1339268|1=Chunfen}} and {{ill|lt=autumnal equinox|Q1200436|WD=Q1200436|1=Qiufen}}, the sun sets at the summit on the extension line of the torii and shrine building. [[ファイル:Higanshinokurashrine.jpg|サムネイル|Sunset over Mt. Iiburi as seen from Shinokura Shrine]] === Access === *15 minutes on foot south from {{ill|lt=Echizen-Ōno Station|Q2815910|WD=Q2815910|1=Echizen-Ōno Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kuzuryū Line|Q5357982|WD=Q5357982|1=Etsumi-Hoku Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}}. *From Echizen-Ōno Station, get off at the “Shinokura Shrine Sandō-guchi” stop on {{ill|lt=Ōno municipal bus #Blue Bus|Q108060249|WD=Q108060249|1=Ōno municipal bus #Blue Bus}}, then immediate walk. *From {{ill|lt=Fukui Station (Fukui)|Q867992|WD=Q867992|lt=Fukui Station|1=Fukui Station (Fukui)}} or {{ill|lt=Katsuyama Station|Q11400269|WD=Q11400269|1=Katsuyama Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keifuku Bus|Q6383575|WD=Q6383575|1=Keifuku Bus}} and get off at “Vio,” then 5 minutes on foot east. === External links === *[http://www.shinokura.net/ Shinokura Shrine] (official site) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinokura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinokura Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:History of Ono, Fukui]] [[Category:大野市の建築物]]
